abort.single_instance_allowed: Only a single instance is allowed.

user.description: If you need help with the configuration have a look at our [GitHub page]({github_url})
user.title: Bermuda BLE Trilateration

abort.no_scanners: Every known scanner already has a calibration offset.

data_description.rssi_offset: Added to this scanner's RSSI before distance is computed.
Added to this scanner's RSSI before distance is computed. Positive = treated as stronger/closer.

user.description: Add a dB offset for a scanner so its readings line up with the others.
user.title: Per-scanner RSSI offset

not_loaded.message: Bermuda is not loaded. Set up the integration before calling this service.

scanner_without_area.description:
Bermuda requires all bluetooth proxies to have a valid area assigned in order to work correctly. Assign an area by visiting each proxy's device page (via `Devices and Services`), and click the pencil icon in the top-right to assign an area. The following proxies are missing an area assignment:

{scannerlist}
scanner_without_area.title: Some Bluetooth Proxies don't have an AREA

error.err_scanner_no_record: The selected scanner hasn't (yet) seen this device.
error.no_devices:
No bluetooth devices are actively being reported from your scanners. 
You will need to solve this before Bermuda can be of much help.
error.no_scanners:
You need to configure some bluetooth scanners before Bermuda will have anything to work with. 
Any one of esphome bluetooth_proxy, Shelly bluetooth proxy or local bluetooth adaptor should get you started.
error.some_active: You have at least some active devices, this is good.

data_description.area_entities: Entities whose area is used while they are on.
data_description.area_entity_distance: Distance the triggered area competes at; lower beats more BLE readings. Default 0.1 m.

area_entities.description: Pick Home Assistant entities (motion, occupancy, contact, ...) whose area should reinforce or override BLE detection. When an entity is on, its area competes with BLE at a 'virtual distance' (smaller = stronger override).
area_entities.title: Area presence entities

area_entities_distance.description:
Set a virtual distance (metres) per entity — smaller wins over BLE more easily. Grouped by area:

{area_summary}
area_entities_distance.title: Per-entity virtual distance

globalopts.description: Tune the global distance model, device-tracking timeouts and RSSI smoothing. These apply to every tracked device.

data.attenuation: Attenuation - Environment attenuation factor for distance calculation/calibration.
data.max_area_radius: Max radius in metres for simple AREA detection
data.ref_power: Reference Power - Default rssi at 1 metre distance, for distance calibration.

data_description.attenuation: After setting ref_power at 1 metre, adjust attenuation so that other distances read correctly - more or less.
data_description.max_area_radius: In the simple `AREA` feature, a device will be marked as being in the AREA of it's closest receiver, if inside this radius. If you set it small, devices will go to `unknown` between receivers, but if large devices will always appear as in their closest Area.
data_description.ref_power: Put your most-common beacon 1 metre (3.28') away from your most-common proxy / scanner. Adjust ref_power until the distance sensor shows a lowest (not average) distance of 1 metre.

distance_model.description: Reference power, attenuation and max radius — the RSSI-to-distance relationship.
distance_model.name: Distance model

data.max_velocity: Max Velocity in metres per second - ignore readings that imply movement away faster than this limit. 3m/s (10km/h) is good.
data.smoothing_samples: Smoothing Samples - how many samples to use for smoothing distance readings.

data_description.max_velocity: If a reading implies a device is moving away faster than this, we ignore that reading. Humans normally walk at 1.4m/s, if they're holding scissors they move at 3m/s.
data_description.smoothing_samples: How many samples to average distance smoothing. Bigger numbers make for slower distance increases. Shortening distances are not affected. 10 or 20 seems good.

smoothing.description: Sample count and velocity clamp that stabilise distance readings.
smoothing.name: RSSI smoothing

data.devtracker_nothome_timeout: Devtracker Timeout in seconds to consider a device as `Not Home`.
data.update_interval: Update Interval - How often (in seconds) to update sensor readings.

data_description.devtracker_nothome_timeout: How quickly to mark device_tracker entities as `not_home` after we stop seeing advertisements. 30 to 300 seconds is probably good.
data_description.update_interval: Shortening distances will still trigger immediately, but increasing distances will be rate limited by this to reduce how much your database grows.

tracking.description: How long until a device is reported Not Home, and how often sensors refresh.
tracking.name: Device tracking

globalopts.title: Global settings

init.description:
Bermuda can currently see:
- {device_counter_active} active out of {device_counter_devices} bluetooth devices.
- {scanner_counter_active} active out of {scanner_counter_scanners} bluetooth scanner devices.

{status}
init.title: Configure Bermuda

data_description.devices: Pick the BLE devices Bermuda should track. Type to search. iBeacons and random MACs are labelled; devices you track but that aren't currently seen show as (saved).
data_description.exclude: Never track these, even if they match a tracked category.
data_description.track_categories: Auto-track every device matching a selected category (e.g. all iBeacons or all named devices).

selectdevices.description: Bermuda only creates tracker entities for the devices you select here. If the list is empty, no Bluetooth devices are being seen yet.
selectdevices.title: Select Devices

calibrate_location.description: Record a tracked device's current spot as a named micro-location (eg 'Key hook'), by snapshotting the RF fingerprint from every proxy that can hear it. Re-running with the same device and name re-calibrates that spot.

area.description: Optional Home Assistant area to associate with this spot. Defaults to the device's current area.
area.name: Area

device.description: The tracked device/item to calibrate. A device name, MAC/UUID, or Home Assistant device id.
device.name: Device

name.description: The name for this spot, eg 'Key hook' or 'Sidetable drawer'.
name.name: Location name

calibrate_location.name: Calibrate micro-location

dump_devices.description: Returns the internal data structure, optionally limited to the given address(es). Includes the rssi and other info from each scanner

addresses.description: An optional space-separated list of MAC addresses to return info on. If blank get all addresses.
addresses.name: Addresses

configured_devices.description: Select to include only scanners and configured devices in the output.
configured_devices.name: Configured Devices

redact.description: Set to TRUE to ensure MAC addresses are redacted in output for privacy.
redact.name: Redact

dump_devices.name: Dump Devices

get_config.description: Return Bermuda's current calibration, tracked devices, scanners and micro-location count.
get_config.name: Get configuration

list_locations.description: List saved micro-locations, optionally filtered to one device.

device.description: Optional device to filter by. Leave blank to list all saved spots.
device.name: Device

list_locations.name: List micro-locations

remove_location.description: Delete a device's saved micro-location by name.

device.description: The device the spot belongs to.
device.name: Device

name.description: The name of the spot to remove.
name.name: Location name

remove_location.name: Remove micro-location

device.description: The device the spot belongs to.
device.name: Device

name.description: The current name of the spot.
name.name: Current name

new_name.description: The new name for the spot.
new_name.name: New name

set_global_calibration.description: Set the global reference power and/or attenuation used for distance estimates.

attenuation.description: Environmental attenuation factor used beyond 1 metre.
attenuation.name: Attenuation

ref_power.description: RSSI (dBm) measured at 1 metre. More negative means shorter estimated distances.
ref_power.name: Reference power

set_global_calibration.name: Set global calibration

set_scanner_offset.description: Set a per-scanner RSSI offset (the per-proxy calibration step, as a service).

rssi_offset.description: Offset added to this scanner's RSSI. Negative increases distance, positive decreases it.
rssi_offset.name: RSSI offset

scanner.description: The scanner/proxy to adjust. A scanner name or address.
scanner.name: Scanner

set_scanner_offset.name: Set scanner RSSI offset

track_device.description: Add a Bluetooth device to Bermuda's tracked devices (creates its sensors).

device.description: The device to track. A device name, MAC/UUID, or Home Assistant device id.
device.name: Device

track_device.name: Track device

untrack_device.description: Remove a device from Bermuda's tracked devices.
untrack_device.name: Untrack device

where_is.description: Report a device's current area and matched micro-location, with confidence.

device.description: The device/item to locate. A device name, MAC/UUID, or Home Assistant device id.
device.name: Device

where_is.name: Where is
